entries.name: nnn_opener.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: file openers (nnn, nano, tmux)
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

    # Get the MIME type of the file
    FPATH="$1"
    MIMETYPE="$(file -bL --mime-type -- "${FPATH}")"

    case "${MIMETYPE}" in
        # Text files and similar
        text/*|application/json|application/xml|application/x-shellscript|inode/x-empty|\
        application/x-yaml|application/javascript)
            if [ -w "$1" ]; then
                tmux new-window -n "edit" "$EDITOR \"$1\""
            else
                tmux new-window -n "edit" "sudo $EDITOR \"$1\""
            fi
        ;;

        # Images
        image/*)
            if type viu >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                tmux new-window -n "image" "viu \"$1\"; read -n 1"
            elif type chafa >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                tmux new-window -n "image" "chafa \"$1\"; read -n 1"
            else
                echo "No terminal image viewer found. Install viu or chafa"
                exit 1
            fi
        ;;

        # PDFs
        application/pdf)
            if type pdftotext >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                tmux new-window -n "pdf" "pdftotext \"$1\" - | less"
            elif type mutool >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                tmux new-window -n "pdf" "mutool draw -F txt \"$1\" | less"
            else
                echo "No PDF viewer found. Install poppler-utils or mupdf-tools"
                exit 1
            fi
        ;;

        # Everything else
        *)
            echo "Cannot open file type: ${MIMETYPE}"
            exit 1
        ;;
    esac

entries.name: nnnopener.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: file openers (nnn, nano, tmux)
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Define a log file path
LOG_FILE="/tmp/nnn-sudo-opener-log.txt"

# Function to log messages with timestamp
log_message() {
    echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - $1" >> "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_message "Script called with argument: '$1'"

# Function to start nano in a new tmux window
start_nano_in_new_window() {
    local file="$1"

    # Create a new window for nano or sudo nano and switch to it
    log_message "Attempting to create a new tmux window for file: '$file'"
    if [ -w "$file" ]; then
        tmux new-window -n "nano" "nano '$file'"
    else
        tmux new-window -n "nano" "sudo nano '$file'"
    fi
    log_message "Opened file: '$file' in a new tmux window"
}

# Check if the argument is empty
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    log_message "No argument received"
else
    # Start nano in a new tmux window, keeping sudo functionality
    start_nano_in_new_window "$1"
fi

entries.name: sudonano.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: file openers (nnn, nano, tmux)
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Define a log file path
LOG_FILE="/tmp/nnn-sudo-opener-log.txt"
#echo "Script invoked by user $(whoami) with args: $@" >> "$LOG_FILE"
#env >> "$LOG_FILE"

# Log the received argument
echo "Script called with argument: '$1'" >> "$LOG_FILE"

# Check if the argument is empty
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    echo "No argument received" >> "$LOG_FILE"
else
    # Attempt to open the file with sudo if not writable
    if [ -w "$1" ]; then
        # If the file is writable, open normally
        nano "$1"
        echo "Successfully opened '$1' with nano." >> "$LOG_FILE"
    else
        # If the file is not writable, attempt to open with sudo
        sudo nano "$1"
        if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "Successfully opened '$1' with sudo nano." >> "$LOG_FILE"
        else
            echo "Failed to open '$1' with sudo nano." >> "$LOG_FILE"
        fi
    fi
fi

entries.name: openvpnup.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: VPN and network status
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

NAMESPACE="vpn"

# Dynamically find the VPN interface name that matches the pattern "tun"
VPN_INTERFACE=$(sudo ip netns exec $NAMESPACE ip link | grep -oE 'tun[0-9]+' | head -n 1)

# Check for the port forwarding script process
PORT_FORWARDING_PROCESS=$(sudo pgrep -f 'port_forwarding.sh')

if [ -n "$VPN_INTERFACE" ] && [ -n "$PORT_FORWARDING_PROCESS" ]; then
    # Check if the VPN interface has an IP address assigned and the port forwarding script is running
    if sudo ip netns exec $NAMESPACE ip addr show $VPN_INTERFACE | grep -q "inet "; then
        echo "#[fg=green]OPENVPN#[default]"  # VPN is up and port forwarding script is running
    else
        echo "#[fg=red]OPENVPN#[default]"  # VPN is down or not correctly configured, or port forwarding script is not running
    fi
else
    echo "#[fg=red]OPENVPN#[default]"  # VPN interface not found or port forwarding script is not running
fi

entries.name: tailscaleup.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: VPN and network status
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Check if the Tailscale interface is up
if ip link show tailscale0 &> /dev/null; then
    # Check if the interface has an IP address assigned
    if ip addr show tailscale0 | grep -q "inet "; then
        # Output "UP" in green
        echo "#[fg=green]Tailscale#[default]"
    else
        # Output "DOWN" in red
        echo "#[fg=red]Tailscale#[default]"
    fi
else
    # Output "DOWN" in red
    echo "#[fg=red]Tailscale#[default]"
fi

entries.name: vpn-helper.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: VPN and network status
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Check if tmux is installed
if command -v tmux >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Check if we're currently in a tmux session
    if [ -z "$TMUX" ]; then
        # Attempt to attach to an existing session named "vpn"
        tmux has-session -t vpn 2>/dev/null
        if [ $? != 0 ]; then
            sudo tmux new-session -d -s vpn 'bash -i /vault/scripts/vpntransmission.sh'
        else
            tmux attach-session -t vpn
        fi
    fi
else
    echo "tmux is not installed. Please install tmux to use this feature."
fi

entries.name: new_websitetile.py
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: website portal and tiles
entries.body:
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import json
import subprocess

def update_apache_config(program_name, port):
    config_file = '/etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf'
    with open(config_file, 'r') as f:
        lines = f.readlines()

    new_proxy_config = f"""
    # Proxying {program_name} service
    ProxyPass /{program_name.lower()}/ http://192.168.123.1:{port}/
    ProxyPassReverse /{program_name.lower()}/ http://192.168.123.1:{port}/
"""

    # Find the position to insert the new configuration
    insert_index = None
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if "ErrorDocument 403 /not-accessible.html" in line:
            insert_index = i + 1
            break

    if insert_index is not None:
        lines.insert(insert_index, new_proxy_config)
    else:
        print("Warning: Couldn't find the ErrorDocument line. Appending to the end of the file.")
        lines.append(new_proxy_config)

    with open(config_file, 'w') as f:
        f.writelines(lines)

def update_registration(program_name):
    reg_file = '/home/anon/registration.txt'
    with open(reg_file, 'r') as f:
        content = json.load(f)

    content.append({"name": program_name, "enabled": True})

    with open(reg_file, 'w') as f:
        json.dump(content, f, indent=2)

def update_ports(program_name, port):
    ports_file = '/home/anon/statics/ports.json'
    with open(ports_file, 'r') as f:
        ports = json.load(f)

    ports[program_name] = port

    with open(ports_file, 'w') as f:
        json.dump(ports, f, indent=2)

def main():
    program_name = input("Enter the program name: ")
    port = input("Enter the port number: ")

    update_apache_config(program_name, port)
    update_registration(program_name)
    update_ports(program_name, int(port))

    # Reload Apache
    subprocess.run(["sudo", "systemctl", "reload", "apache2"])

    print(f"New tile for {program_name} has been added successfully!")
    print(f"Don't forget to add an image named {program_name.lower()}.jpg to /var/www/html/images/")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

entries.name: newtileforwebsite.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: website portal and tiles
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Prompt for program name and port
read -p "Enter the program name: " program_name
read -p "Enter the port number: " port_number

# Convert program name to lowercase for use in URLs
program_name_lower=$(echo "$program_name" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')

# Update Apache configuration
echo "Updating Apache configuration..."
sudo sed -i "/<VirtualHost \*:80>/,/<\/VirtualHost>/ {
    /# Proxying.*service/!b
    :a
    n
    /ProxyPassReverse/!ba
    a\
\n    # Proxying $program_name service\n    ProxyPass /$program_name_lower/ http://192.168.123.1:$port_number/\n    ProxyPassReverse /$program_name_lower/ http://192.168.123.1:$port_number/
}" /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf

# Update registration.txt
echo "Updating registration.txt..."
sudo sed -i "$ s/]$/,\n  { \"name\": \"$program_name\", \"enabled\": true }\n]/" /home/anon/registration.txt

# Update ports.json
echo "Updating ports.json..."
sudo sed -i "$ s/}$/,\n  \"$program_name\": $port_number\n}/" /home/anon/statics/ports.json

# Reload Apache
echo "Reloading Apache..."
sudo systemctl reload apache2

echo "New tile for $program_name has been added successfully!"
echo "Don't forget to add an image named ${program_name_lower}.jpg to /var/www/html/images/"

entries.name: update_website_port.py
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: website portal and tiles
entries.body:
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import json
import subprocess

def update_apache_config(program_name, port):
    config_file = '/etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf'
    with open(config_file, 'r') as f:
        lines = f.readlines()

    proxy_pattern = f"""
    # Proxying {program_name} service
    ProxyPass /{program_name.lower()}/ http://192.168.123.1:{port}/
    ProxyPassReverse /{program_name.lower()}/ http://192.168.123.1:{port}/
"""

    start_index = None
    end_index = None

    # Find the existing configuration block for the selected program
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if f"# Proxying {program_name} service" in line:
            start_index = i
        if start_index and line.strip() == "":
            end_index = i
            break

    if start_index is not None and end_index is not None:
        lines = lines[:start_index] + lines[end_index:]
        insert_index = start_index
    else:
        for i, line in enumerate(lines):
            if "ErrorDocument 403 /not-accessible.html" in line:
                insert_index = i + 1
                break
        if insert_index is None:
            print("Warning: Couldn't find the ErrorDocument line. Appending to the end of the file.")
            insert_index = len(lines)

    lines.insert(insert_index, proxy_pattern)

    with open(config_file, 'w') as f:
        f.writelines(lines)

def update_ports(program_name, port):
    ports_file = '/home/anon/statics/ports.json'
    with open(ports_file, 'r') as f:
        ports = json.load(f)

    ports[program_name] = port

    with open(ports_file, 'w') as f:
        json.dump(ports, f, indent=2)

def list_programs():
    reg_file = '/home/anon/registration.txt'
    with open(reg_file, 'r') as f:
        content = json.load(f)

    print("Available programs:")
    for i, entry in enumerate(content):
        enabled_status = entry.get('enabled', 'Unknown')
        print(f"{i + 1}. {entry['name']} (Enabled: {enabled_status})")

    return content

def main():
    content = list_programs()
    program_index = int(input("Enter the number of the program you want to update: ")) - 1

    if program_index < 0 or program_index >= len(content):
        print("Invalid program number selected.")
        return

    program_name = content[program_index]['name']
    new_port = input(f"Enter the new port number for {program_name}: ")

    update_apache_config(program_name, new_port)
    update_ports(program_name, int(new_port))

    # Reload Apache
    subprocess.run(["sudo", "systemctl", "reload", "apache2"])

    print(f"Configuration for {program_name} has been updated successfully!")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

entries.name: addnavidrome.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: website portal and tiles
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Exit on any error
set -e

# Variables
NAVIDROME_VERSION="0.53.1"  # Replace with the latest version
NAVIDROME_BINARY="/usr/local/bin/navidrome"
NAVIDROME_CONFIG="/etc/navidrome/navidrome.toml"
NAVIDROME_DATA="/var/lib/navidrome"
MUSIC_FOLDER="/mnt/elements/music"
NAVIDROME_USER="anon"
NAVIDROME_PORT=7777

# Function to check if a command exists
command_exists() {
    command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

# Check if running as root
if [ "$(id -u)" != "0" ]; then
   echo "This script must be run as root" 1>&2
   exit 1
fi

# Install dependencies
apt update
apt install -y curl sqlite3

# Download and install Navidrome
echo "Downloading Navidrome..."
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://github.com/navidrome/navidrome/releases/download/v${NAVIDROME_VERSION}/navidrome_${NAVIDROME_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz"
if ! curl -L -o /tmp/navidrome.tar.gz "$DOWNLOAD_URL"; then
    echo "Failed to download Navidrome. Please check the version and your internet connection."
    exit 1
fi

# Add verbose output for debugging
echo "Download completed. Extracting archive..."
tar -xzvf /tmp/navidrome.tar.gz -C /tmp

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Failed to extract Navidrome archive. The downloaded file might be corrupted."
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f /tmp/navidrome ]; then
    echo "Navidrome binary not found in the extracted archive."
    exit 1
fi

mv /tmp/navidrome $NAVIDROME_BINARY
chmod +x $NAVIDROME_BINARY

# Create necessary directories
mkdir -p /etc/navidrome
mkdir -p $NAVIDROME_DATA
chown $NAVIDROME_USER:$NAVIDROME_USER $NAVIDROME_DATA

# Create Navidrome configuration
cat << EOF > $NAVIDROME_CONFIG
MusicFolder = "$MUSIC_FOLDER"
DataFolder = "$NAVIDROME_DATA"
Port = $NAVIDROME_PORT
Address = "127.0.0.1"
EOF

# Create systemd service file
cat << EOF > /etc/systemd/system/navidrome.service
[Unit]
Description=Navidrome Music Server and Streamer
After=remote-fs.target network.target
AssertPathExists=/var/lib/navidrome

[Service]
User=$NAVIDROME_USER
Group=$NAVIDROME_USER
Type=simple
ExecStart=$NAVIDROME_BINARY --configfile "$NAVIDROME_CONFIG"
WorkingDirectory=$NAVIDROME_DATA
KillMode=process
Restart=on-failure
ProtectHome=true

DevicePolicy=closed
NoNewPrivileges=yes
PrivateTmp=yes
PrivateUsers=yes
ProtectControlGroups=yes
ProtectKernelModules=yes
ProtectKernelTunables=yes
RestrictAddressFamilies=AF_UNIX AF_INET AF_INET6
RestrictNamespaces=yes
RestrictRealtime=yes
SystemCallFilter=~@clock @debug @module @mount @obsolete @reboot @setuid @swap
ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/navidrome

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Reload systemd, enable and start Navidrome
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable navidrome
systemctl start navidrome

# Add Navidrome to the web portal using new_websitetile.py
echo "Adding Navidrome to the web portal..."
python3 /home/anon/scripts/new_websitetile.py <<EOF
Navidrome
$NAVIDROME_PORT
EOF

# Download Navidrome logo
curl -L "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/navidrome/navidrome/master/resources/logo.png" -o /var/www/html/images/navidrome.jpg

echo "Navidrome installation complete and added to the web portal!"
echo "Please ensure you have the correct music files in $MUSIC_FOLDER"
echo "You can access Navidrome at http://your-server-ip:$NAVIDROME_PORT"

entries.name: setup_gogs.sh
entries.sourceTitle: Serverbox scripts: website portal and tiles
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Install Gogs
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y gogs

# Configure Gogs
sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/gogs
sudo chown -R anon:anon /var/lib/gogs

# Create a systemd service file for Gogs
cat << EOF | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/gogs.service
[Unit]
Description=Gogs
After=syslog.target
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=anon
Group=anon
WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/gogs/
ExecStart=/usr/bin/gogs web
Restart=always
Environment=USER=anon HOME=/home/anon

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Start and enable Gogs service
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl start gogs
sudo systemctl enable gogs

# Add Gogs to your website
sudo python3 /home/anon/scripts/new_websitetile.py << EOF
Gogs
1730
EOF

# Add Gogs image
sudo mv /share/gogs.jpg /var/www/html/images/gogs.jpg

echo "Gogs has been installed and configured."

entries.name: check_updates.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Function to check for updates
check_updates() {
  # Run your update check command here, e.g.:
  sudo apt update > /dev/null 2>&1
  updates=$(sudo apt list --upgradable 2>/dev/null | grep -c upgradable)
  echo $updates > /var/www/html/update_count
  echo $updates
}

# Function to apply updates
apply_updates() {
  # Run your update command here, e.g.:
update
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo 0 > /var/www/html/update_count
    echo "success"
  else
    echo "error"
  fi
}

# Main execution
if [ "$1" == "check" ]; then
  check_updates
elif [ "$1" == "apply" ]; then
  apply_updates
else
  echo "Invalid argument. Use 'check' or 'apply'."
fi

entries.name: technitium.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Variables
SERVER="localhost:5380"
USERNAME="admin"
PASSWORD="Escort2312"

# Function to check if command was successful
check_success() {
  if [ "$1" != "ok" ]; then
    echo "Error: $2"
    exit 1
  fi
}

# Step 1: Login and get session token
RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST "http://$SERVER/api/user/login?user=$USERNAME&pass=$PASSWORD")
STATUS=$(echo $RESPONSE | jq -r '.status')
check_success "$STATUS" "Login failed."

TOKEN=$(echo $RESPONSE | jq -r '.token')
if [ "$TOKEN" == "null" ]; then
  echo "Failed to obtain token. Exiting."
  exit 1
fi
echo "Obtained Token: $TOKEN"

# Step 2: Check for update
UPDATE_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X GET "http://$SERVER/api/user/checkForUpdate?token=$TOKEN")
UPDATE_CHECK=$(echo $UPDATE_RESPONSE | jq -r '.response.updateAvailable')
UPDATE_STATUS=$(echo $UPDATE_RESPONSE | jq -r '.status')
check_success "$UPDATE_STATUS" "Failed to check for updates."

# Step 3: Conditionally apply update
if [ "$UPDATE_CHECK" = "true" ]; then
  echo "Update available. Updating now..."
  curl -sSL https://download.technitium.com/dns/install.sh | sudo bash
else
  echo "No update available."
fi

# Step 4: Logout
LOGOUT_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST "http://$SERVER/api/user/logout?token=$TOKEN")
LOGOUT_STATUS=$(echo $LOGOUT_RESPONSE | jq -r '.status')
check_success "$LOGOUT_STATUS" "Logout failed."

entries.name: everything.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Function to log messages and run updates
function update() {
    local script_name="$1"
    echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - Starting update for $script_name..."
    ~/scripts/updates/${script_name}.sh

    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - Update completed successfully for $script_name."
    else
        echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - Update failed for $script_name."
    fi
}


# Call the update function for each script
update "atuin"
# Add more updates as needed

entries.name: atuin.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Function to send notification
send_notification() {
    local message="$1"
    echo "$message" | mail -s "Atuin Update Error" user@example.com
}

# Fetch the latest version tag from GitHub
response=$(curl -s -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/latest_atuin_release.json https://api.github.com/repos/username/atuin/releases/latest)
http_status=$(tail -n1 /tmp/latest_atuin_release.json) # Get the last line which contains HTTP status
latest_version=$(cat /tmp/latest_atuin_release.json | grep 'tag_name' | cut -d '"' -f 4)

# Check HTTP response
if [ "$http_status" != "200" ]; then
    send_notification "Failed to fetch latest Atuin release: HTTP status $http_status."
    exit 1
fi

# Get current installed version
current_version=$(atuin --version 2>/dev/null)
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    send_notification "Atuin is not installed properly or the version cannot be checked."
    exit 1
fi

# Compare versions and update if there's a new one
if [ "$latest_version" != "$current_version" ]; then
    wget https://github.com/username/atuin/releases/download/$latest_version/atuin-$latest_version.deb -O /tmp/atuin-$latest_version.deb
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        send_notification "Failed to download Atuin $latest_version."
        exit 1
    fi

    sudo dpkg -i /tmp/atuin-$latest_version.deb
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        send_notification "Failed to install Atuin $latest_version."
        exit 1
    fi
else
    echo "Atuin is up to date."
fi

entries.name: btrfsdelete.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# List all subvolumes
btrfs subvolume list /

# Delete all snapshots (BE VERY CAREFUL WITH THIS!)
btrfs subvolume list / | grep -i snapshot | awk '{print $NF}' | while read subvol; do
    btrfs subvolume delete "/$subvol"
done

# Verify that snapshots are deleted
btrfs subvolume list /

entries.name: create_filtered_symlinks.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

source_dir="/mnt/elements/catalog"
target_dir="/var/www/piwigo/galleries/filtered_catalog"

find "$source_dir" -type f ! -name '*poster*' -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
    relative_path="${file#$source_dir/}"
    
    # Truncate the path if it's too long
    if [ ${#relative_path} -gt 450 ]; then
        filename=$(basename "$relative_path")
        directory=$(dirname "$relative_path")
        truncated_dir=$(echo "$directory" | cut -c1-400)
        relative_path="${truncated_dir}/${filename}"
    fi
    
    target_file="$target_dir/$relative_path"
    target_dir_path=$(dirname "$target_file")
    
    mkdir -p "$target_dir_path"
    ln -sf "$file" "$target_file"
done

entries.name: mountdrives.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash
flag_file="/tmp/.anon_mount_done"
LOG_FILE="/tmp/mountdrives.log"
touch $LOG_FILE

# Set the default verbosity level to 2 (ERROR).
VERBOSITY=5
if [[ ! -z "$1" && "$1" =~ ^[0-5]$ ]]; then
    VERBOSITY=$1
fi

log_message() {
    local level=$1
    local message=$2

    # Convert log level to a human-readable string and decide log action
    local level_str=""
    case $level in
        5) level_str="DEBUG";; # everything else 
        4) level_str="VARIABLES";; # after a variable is set, output its variable
        3) level_str="IMMA";; # indicating a command is is about to be ran ; imma attempt to mount the drives . .
        2) level_str="SUCCESS";; # if a command was successfully completed
        1) level_str="ERROR";; # errors are explicitly marked as "ERROR".
        0) level_str="STATUS";; # Always logged, providing constant feedback as to the status of the script.
    esac

    # Log messages according to the set verbosity level or specific rules for STATUS and ERROR.
    if [ $level -le $VERBOSITY ] || [ "$level_str" = "STATUS" ]; then
        if [[ "$level_str" = "ERROR" || "$level_str" = "STATUS" ]]; then
            # ERROR and STATUS include a timestamp for critical info or constant status updates
            echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - $level_str: $message" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        else
            echo "$level_str: $message" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        fi
    fi
}
# Check if the script has already run this boot cycle
if [ -f "$flag_file" ]; then
    echo "The mount script has already run this boot cycle. Exiting."
    exit 0
fi

read_credentials() {
    local utility_name="$1"
    local key_file="/vault/.keys/${utility_name}.key"

    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        value=$(echo $value | tr -d '"') # Remove quotes from the value
        case "$key" in
            username) declare -g "${utility_name^^}_USERNAME=$value" ;;
            password) declare -g "${utility_name^^}_PASSWORD=$value" ;;
        esac
    done < <(sudo cat "$key_file")
}

# Ensuring the /vault directory exists
if [ ! -d "/vault" ]; then
  sudo mkdir -p /vault
fi


decrypt_and_mount_key_partition() {
  local passphrase
  local attempt=0
  local max_attempts=5
  local success=0

  while [ $attempt -lt $max_attempts ]; do
    # Echo directly to the terminal for immediate feedback
    echo "Attempt $(($attempt + 1)) of $max_attempts."
    # Also log the attempt information
    log_message 0 "Attempt $(($attempt + 1)) of $max_attempts to decrypt /vault."

    read -s -p "Enter passphrase for key partition: " passphrase
    echo

    # Attempt to open the LUKS volume with the provided passphrase
    if echo $passphrase | sudo cryptsetup luksOpen /dev/sda7 vault --key-file - 2>>$LOG_FILE; then
      # Check if /vault is already mounted
      if findmnt -rno SOURCE,TARGET /vault > /dev/null; then
          log_message 0 "/vault is already mounted."
          success=1
          break  # Exit the loop since /vault is already mounted and ready
      else
          log_message 3 "Mounting /vault..."
          if sudo mount /dev/mapper/vault /vault 2>>$LOG_FILE; then
			  echo "Access Granted . . ."
              log_message 2 "/vault mounted successfully."
              success=1
              break  # Exit the loop since we've successfully mounted /vault
          fi
      fi
    else
      log_message 1 "Incorrect passphrase or error decrypting /vault. Please try again."
    fi

    attempt=$(($attempt + 1))
  done

  if [ $success -ne 1 ]; then
    log_message 1 "Failed to decrypt and mount /vault after $max_attempts attempts. Exiting."
    exit 1
  else
    log_message 2 "Successfully decrypted and mounted /vault."
  fi
}

# Function to open and mount a LUKS volume using a keyfile or a VeraCrypt volume using the keyfile as a password
open_and_mount() {
  local dev_path=$1
  local mount_point=$2
  local keyfile=$3

  # Log and echo the attempt to mount
  echo "Mounting $dev_path at $mount_point with keyfile"
  log_message 0 "Mounting $dev_path at $mount_point"

  # Check if the device is a LUKS encrypted volume
  if sudo cryptsetup isLuks $dev_path >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1; then
    log_message 3 "Detected LUKS encryption on $dev_path"
    local mapper_name=$(basename $dev_path)_crypt
    if sudo cryptsetup luksOpen $dev_path $mapper_name --key-file $keyfile >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1; then
      log_message 3 "Mounting $dev_path to $mount_point..."
      if sudo mount /dev/mapper/$mapper_name $mount_point >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1; then
        log_message 2 "$mount_point mounted successfully."
      else
        log_message 1 "Failed to mount $dev_path to $mount_point."
      fi
    else
      log_message 1 "Failed to open LUKS volume on $dev_path."
    fi
  else
    log_message 0 "Mounting via VeraCrypt for $dev_path"
    # Read the password from the keyfile
    local password=$(sudo cat "$keyfile")
    # Attempt to open and mount the VeraCrypt volume using the password
    if sudo veracrypt --text --password="$password" --protect-hidden=no --non-interactive --mount "$dev_path" "$mount_point" >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1; then
      log_message 2 "$mount_point mounted successfully."
    else
      log_message 1 "Failed to mount VeraCrypt volume on $dev_path."
    fi
  fi
}
open_and_mount2() {
  local dev_path="$1"
  local mount_point="$2"
  local keyfile="$3"
  local password=$(sudo cat "$keyfile")
  log_message 4 "password : $password"
  echo "Mounting $dev_path at $mount_point"
  log_message 0 "Mounting $dev_path at $mount_point"

  # Check if the device is a LUKS encrypted volume
  if sudo cryptsetup isLuks "$dev_path" >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1; then
    log_message 3 "Detected LUKS encryption on $dev_path"
    local mapper_name=$(basename "$dev_path")_crypt

    # Attempt to open the LUKS volume using the password
    echo "$password" | sudo cryptsetup luksOpen "$dev_path" "$mapper_name" --key-file - >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
      log_message 3 "Mounting $dev_path to $mount_point..."
      if sudo mount "/dev/mapper/$mapper_name" "$mount_point" >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1; then
        log_message 2 "$mount_point mounted successfully."
      else
        log_message 1 "Failed to mount $dev_path to $mount_point."
        echo "Mounting Failed"
      fi
    else
      log_message 1 "Failed to open LUKS volume on $dev_path."
    fi
  else
    log_message 1 "$dev_path is not a LUKS encrypted volume."
  fi
}


log_message 0 "Mount and Decrypt Script Began."
echo "Please enter your sudo password to continue:"
sudo -v

echo "Initiating decryption and mounting process for the key partition..."
decrypt_and_mount_key_partition

if command -v tmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -z "$TMUX" ]; then
	  # Start a new tmux session or attach to an existing one named "default"
	  tmux attach-session -t default || tmux new-session -s default
fi

echo "Proceeding to mount encrypted volumes with available key files..."

# Attempt to open and mount each encrypted volume using the keyfile
read_credentials "elements"
#open_and_mount /dev/sda6 /dump "/vault/.keys/partitions.key"
#open_and_mount /dev/sda8 /share "/vault/.keys/partitions.key"
open_and_mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/elements "/vault/.keys/elements.key"
open_and_mount /dev/sdc1 /mnt/wd-drive "vault/.keys/elements.key"
#open_and_mount2 /dev/sdb1 /mnt/elements $ELEMENTS_PASSWORD
#open_and_mount2 /dev/sdb1 /mnt/elements "/vault/.keys/elements.key"
#sudo /vault/scripts/elements-auto-mount.sh
touch "$flag_file"
log_message 0 "All drives Mounted and Decrypted."

sudo ~/scripts/vpn-helper.sh

entries.name: unmountdrives.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Function to unmount and lock a LUKS volume
unmount_and_lock() {
  local mount_point=$1
  local mapper_name=$2

  # Check if the mount point is mounted and unmount it
  if mountpoint -q $mount_point; then
    echo "Unmounting $mount_point..."
    sudo umount $mount_point
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
      echo "$mount_point unmounted successfully."
    else
      echo "Failed to unmount $mount_point. It may be in use."
      return 1
    fi
  else
    echo "$mount_point is not mounted."
  fi

  # Attempt to lock the LUKS volume
  echo "Locking $mapper_name..."
  sudo cryptsetup luksClose $mapper_name
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "$mapper_name locked successfully."
  else
    echo "Failed to lock $mapper_name. It may not be open or still in use."
  fi
}

# Attempt to unmount and lock each encrypted volume
unmount_and_lock /dump sda6_crypt
unmount_and_lock /vault sda7_crypt
unmount_and_lock /share sda8_crypt

#!/bin/bash

# Check if the torrent has reached the desired ratio
if [ "$TR_TORRENT_PERCENT_DONE" = "100" ] && [ $(echo "$TR_TORRENT_RATIO >= 3" | bc) -eq 1 ]; then
    # Move the completed file to the complete directory
    mv "$TR_TORRENT_DIR/$TR_TORRENT_NAME" "/mnt/elements/torrents/complete/"

    # Remove the .torrent file from the objectives folder
    rm -f "/mnt/elements/torrents/objectives/$TR_TORRENT_NAME.torrent"
fi#!/bin/bash

# Check if the torrent has reached the desired ratio
if [ "$TR_TORRENT_PERCENT_DONE" = "100" ] && [ $(echo "$TR_TORRENT_RATIO >= 3" | bc) -eq 1 ]; then

#!/bin/bash

# Script parameters provided by Transmission
TORRENT_PATH="$TR_TORRENT_DIR"
TORRENT_NAME="$TR_TORRENT_NAME"

# Define the log file location
LOG_FILE="~/daily_log.txt"

# Get the current date in Year-Month-Day format
DATE=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d')

# Append the completed file information to the log
echo "$DATE: '$TORRENT_NAME' completed." >> "$LOG_FILE"

entries.body:
<?php
require_once __DIR__.'/bootstrap/autoload.php';
$app = require_once __DIR__.'/bootstrap/app.php';
$app->make('Illuminate\Contracts\Console\Kernel')->bootstrap();

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage;

$missingFiles = DB::table('size_variants')
    ->select('id', 'photo_id', 'type', 'short_path')
    ->get()
    ->filter(function ($variant) {
        return !Storage::disk($variant->storage_disk)->exists($variant->short_path);
    });

echo "Missing files count: " . $missingFiles->count() . "\n";
echo "First 10 missing files:\n";
foreach ($missingFiles->take(10) as $file) {
    echo "{$file->id}, {$file->photo_id}, {$file->type}, {$file->short_path}\n";
}

entries.name: configure-tls-cert.sh
entries.body:
#!/bin/bash

# Configuration
CERT_DIR="/etc/mail/certs"
CERT_FILE="sendmail.crt"
KEY_FILE="sendmail.key"
SENDMAIL_CONFIG="/etc/mail/sendmail.mc"
SENDMAIL_GENERATED_CONFIG="/etc/mail/sendmail.cf"

# Function to generate SSL/TLS certificates
generate_certificates() {
    sudo mkdir -p "$CERT_DIR"
    sudo openssl req -new -nodes -x509 -days 365 -keyout "$CERT_DIR/$KEY_FILE" -out "$CERT_DIR/$CERT_FILE"
}

# Function to configure Sendmail
configure_sendmail() {
    sudo sed -i '/^DAEMON_OPTIONS/d' "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG"
    sudo sed -i '/^define(`confCACERT_PATH/d' "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG"
    sudo sed -i '/^define(`confCACERT/d' "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG"
    sudo sed -i '/^define(`confSERVER_CERT/d' "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG"
    sudo sed -i '/^define(`confSERVER_KEY/d' "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG"
    sudo sed -i '/^define(`confTLS_SRV_OPTIONS/d' "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG"

    echo "DAEMON_OPTIONS(\`Port=smtps, Name=TLSMTA, M=s')dnl" | sudo tee -a "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG" > /dev/null
    echo "define(\`confCACERT_PATH', \`$CERT_DIR')dnl" | sudo tee -a "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG" > /dev/null
    echo "define(\`confCACERT', \`$CERT_DIR/$CERT_FILE')dnl" | sudo tee -a "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG" > /dev/null
    echo "define(\`confSERVER_CERT', \`$CERT_DIR/$CERT_FILE')dnl" | sudo tee -a "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG" > /dev/null
    echo "define(\`confSERVER_KEY', \`$CERT_DIR/$KEY_FILE')dnl" | sudo tee -a "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG" > /dev/null
    echo "define(\`confTLS_SRV_OPTIONS', \`V')dnl" | sudo tee -a "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG" > /dev/null
}

# Function to generate Sendmail configuration
generate_sendmail_config() {
    sudo m4 "$SENDMAIL_CONFIG" > "$SENDMAIL_GENERATED_CONFIG"
}

# Function to restart Sendmail
restart_sendmail() {
    sudo systemctl restart sendmail
}

# Main script
echo "Generating SSL/TLS certificates..."
generate_certificates

echo "Configuring Sendmail..."
configure_sendmail

echo "Generating Sendmail configuration..."
generate_sendmail_config

echo "Restarting Sendmail..."
restart_sendmail

echo "SSL/TLS configuration complete."

import subprocess import csv import smtplib import datetime from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart from email.mime.text import MIMEText def get_system_data(command): """Execute a system command and return its output.""" return subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, shell=True).stdout def convert_timestamp_to_date(timestamp): """Convert Unix timestamp to human-readable date.""" return datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(int(timestamp)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') def get_uptime(): """Get the system uptime.""" return get_system_data("uptime") # Gather data data_usage = get_system_data("vnstat") disk_usage = get_system_data("df -h /mnt/elements") update_command = "apt-get update > /dev/null 2>&1 && apt-get -s upgrade" update_output = get_system_data(update_command) uptime_info = get_uptime() # Parse update list updates = [line for line in update_output.splitlines() if line.startswith("Inst")] update_rows = [] for update in updates: parts = update.split() package = parts[1] current_version = parts[2].strip('[]') new_version = parts[3].strip('()') update_rows.append([package, current_version, new_version]) # Create HTML for updates table headers = ["Package", "Current Version", "New Version"] html_table_updates = "" + "".join(f"" for header in headers) + "" for row in update_rows: html_table_updates += "" + "".join(f"" for cell in row) + "" html_table_updates += "
{header}
{cell}
" # Read lease files lease_files = ["/var/lib/kea/kea-leases4.csv", "/var/lib/kea/kea-leases4.csv.2"] device_info = set() # Use a set to store unique device details for lease_file in lease_files: try: with open(lease_file, newline='') as csvfile: reader = csv.reader(csvfile) next(reader) # skip header for row in reader: host, ip, mac = row[8], row[0], row[1] device_info.add((host, ip, mac)) # Add device details as a tuple to the set except FileNotFoundError: print(f"Lease file not found: {lease_file}") # Create HTML for device table device_headers = ["Hostname", "IP Address", "MAC Address"] html_table_devices = "" + "".join(f"" for header in device_headers) + "" for device in device_info: html_table_devices += "" + "".join(f"" for cell in device) + "" html_table_devices += "
{header}
{cell}
" # Format the email content html = f"""\

Data Usage:

{data_usage}

Disk Usage:

{disk_usage}

Pending Updates:

{html_table_updates}

Device Information:

{html_table_devices}

Uptime Information:

{uptime_info}
""" # Setup email message msg = MIMEMultipart('alternative') msg['Subject'] = 'Daily Server Report' msg['From'] = 'your_email@example.com' msg['To'] = 'coltinengle@gmail.com' part = MIMEText(html, 'html') msg.attach(part) # Send the message via local SMTP server with smtplib.SMTP('localhost') as smtp: smtp.send_message(msg) print("Email sent successfully!")

entries.name: temp.py
cpu.py
entries.body:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess
import time
from email_utils import send_email, format_html_email

def get_cpu_usage():
    command = "top -bn1 | grep 'Cpu(s)'"
    output = subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, shell=True).stdout
    return output

def parse_cpu_usage(cpu_output):
    usage_parts = cpu_output.replace("%Cpu(s):", "").strip().split(',')
    usage_dict = {}
    for part in usage_parts:
        key_value = part.strip().split()
        usage_dict[key_value[1]] = float(key_value[0])
    user_usage = usage_dict['us']
    system_usage = usage_dict['sy']
    return user_usage, system_usage

def get_top_processes():
    command = "ps -eo pid,pcpu,comm --sort=-pcpu | grep -v 'ps' | head -n 4"
    output = subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, shell=True).stdout
    processes = output.strip().split('\n')[1:]
    return processes

def write_to_log(message):
    with open('/tmp/cpu.log', 'a') as log_file:
        log_file.write(message + '\n')

def monitor_cpu_usage(threshold=90.0, duration_threshold=60):
    high_usage_start = None
    event_duration = 0
    top_processes_event = []
    while True:
        cpu_usage = get_cpu_usage()
        user_usage, system_usage = parse_cpu_usage(cpu_usage)
        total_usage = user_usage + system_usage
        write_to_log(f"Current CPU Usage: User: {user_usage}% | System: {system_usage}% | Total: {total_usage}%")
        
        if total_usage > threshold:
            write_to_log("High CPU usage detected.")
            if high_usage_start is None:
                write_to_log("Starting hysteresis check...")
                # Perform hysteresis check
                hysteresis_count = 0
                for _ in range(5):
                    cpu_usage = get_cpu_usage()
                    user_usage, system_usage = parse_cpu_usage(cpu_usage)
                    total_usage = user_usage + system_usage
                    if total_usage > threshold:
                        hysteresis_count += 1
                    time.sleep(1)
                
                if hysteresis_count >= 3:
                    # Confirm high usage for at least 3 out of 5 seconds
                    high_usage_start = time.time()  # Start the timer
                    write_to_log(f"High CPU usage event started at {time.ctime(high_usage_start)}")
                    
                    subject = "High CPU Usage Alert"
                    message = "Current Usage:\n"
                    
                    table_data = [["User", f"{user_usage}%"], ["System", f"{system_usage}%"], ["Total", f"{total_usage}%"]]
                    
                    html_email = format_html_email('', '', table_data=table_data)
                    send_email(subject, html_email)
                else:
                    write_to_log("Hysteresis check failed. High CPU usage not confirmed.")
            else:
                write_to_log("High CPU usage event in progress.")
                event_duration = time.time() - high_usage_start
                top_processes_event = get_top_processes()
            time.sleep(2)  # Poll every other second
        else:
            write_to_log("CPU usage is below the threshold.")
            if high_usage_start is not None:
                event_duration += time.time() - high_usage_start
                write_to_log(f"High CPU usage event ended. Total duration: {event_duration} seconds")

                if event_duration >= duration_threshold:
                    subject = "CPU Usage Normalized"
                    title = f"High CPU usage lasted for {int(event_duration)} seconds."
                    message = f"Top 3 processes that caused high CPU usage:"
                    
                    table_data = [["PID", "CPU Usage", "Command"]]
                    for process in top_processes_event:
                        pid, cpu, comm = process.split(maxsplit=2)
                        table_data.append([pid, cpu + "%", comm])
                    
                    html_email = format_html_email(title, message, title_heading_level=2, message_italic=True, table_data=table_data)
                    send_email(subject, html_email)
                    write_to_log(f"Followup email sent. Duration: {int(event_duration)} seconds")
                else:
                    write_to_log(f"High CPU usage event ended, but total duration ({event_duration} seconds) is below the threshold.")
                
                high_usage_start = None  # Reset the timer
                event_duration = 0  # Reset the event duration
                top_processes_event = []  # Reset the top processes list
            time.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds if usage is below threshold

monitor_cpu_usage()
